incident_summary: API gateway experienced severe latency degradation (p99 15.2s vs normal <1s) for approximately 2.5 hours due to database connection pool exhaustion caused by a configuration change that tripled HTTP client timeout values.
root_cause_summary: HTTP client timeout configuration change from 10s to 30s caused database connection pool exhaustion by holding connections 3x longer than normal, preventing connection recycling.
root_cause_trigger: Deployment at 2025-01-15T14:15:00Z changing HTTP client timeout from 10s to 30s
customer_impact_description: Customers experienced severe API performance degradation with response times increasing from under 1 second to over 15 seconds, causing timeouts and intermittent errors when accessing the platform.
affected_functionality: API requests; Platform access via API
resolution_action: Rolled back the HTTP client timeout configuration change, reverting timeout from 30s back to 10s
resolution_confirmed_by: Connection pool utilization dropped from 100% (50/50) to 60% (30/50) per CloudWatch logs at 15:05 UTC; engineer monitoring confirmed metrics stable for 90 minutes post-rollback before resolving incident
data_gaps: Geographic scope unknown - no data indicates whether impact was global or regional; Percentage of requests affected unknown - logs show errors but not total request volume or error rate; Specific customer-facing features affected unknown beyond general 'API' - no data on which API endpoints or product capabilities were impacted; Baseline metrics incomplete - no pre-incident p99 latency values provided to quantify degradation magnitude; Other services/products status unknown - only api-gateway mentioned, unclear if other services were affected; Customer reports/tickets not included - impact assessment based on technical metrics only
